description_long: This is an old, weathered wooden house with a steep gable roof covered in worn, brown shingles. The exterior walls are faded and peeling, with visible signs of decay and neglect. The front features a small porch with broken railings and steps, and several windows, some of which are missing panes or have shutters askew. The house has a wraparound porch on one side, supported by wooden posts, and the roof shows significant moss and discoloration. The structure appears to be elevated slightly above the ground, with a partially enclosed foundation. Overall, the house exudes a sense of abandonment and rustic charm, typical of early 20th-century rural American homes.
description: A dilapidated wooden house with a steep, shingled gable roof, peeling paint, and a wraparound porch with broken railings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, broken steps, and several windows with damaged frames.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long wraparound porch, weathered railings, and the full expanse of the shingled roof.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the back of the house, the continuation of the porch, and additional roof details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with more of the porch visible, showing extensive roof wear and the house's elevated foundation.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple front porch with broken wooden steps, partial railing, and a plain door; no decorative columns or overhangs.
